testcase.name: test_flux_partition

failure.message: ValueError: buffer source array is read-only

testcase.failure:
def test_flux_partition(): """Integrated test of highest level flux_partition function.
In test below, the "desired" results are not exact or even necessarily correct. They were obtained with an independent (matlab) code, and there should be reasonable agreement. """
wue_data = { "meas_ht": 7.11, "canopy_ht": 4.42, "ppath": "C3", "ci_mod": "const_ppm", "diff_ratio": 1 / 0.7, }
# soln exists for this data without any wavelet filtering fname = os.path.join( TESTDIR, "data/TOA5_6843.ts_Above_2012_06_07_1300.dat" )
matlab_fluxes = SimpleNamespace( Fcp=-1.02807378762793, Fcr=0.402683101177712, Fqe=0.00500869036088203, Fqt=0.145615860044424, Fcp_mol=-23.3600042633021, Fcr_mol=9.14980916104776, LEe=12.1870591763138, LEt=354.310004313924, )
> fvsp = fvs_partition(fname, wue_options=wue_data, hfd_format="ec-TOA5") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_fluxpart.py:47: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ fluxpart/api.py:314: in fvs_partition return fvspart( fluxpart/fluxpart.py:304: in fvspart fluxes, fvsp = fvspart_progressive( fluxpart/partition.py:60: in fvspart_progressive for cnt, lowcut_wqc in enumerate(_progressive_lowcut(w, q, c)): fluxpart/partition.py:421: in _progressive_lowcut for lowcut_series in zip(lowcut_w, lowcut_q, lowcut_c): fluxpart/util.py:253: in progressive_lowcut_series decomp_coef = pywt.wavedec(series_data, wavelet=wavelet, level=nlevel) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/pywt/_multilevel.py:103: in wavedec a, d = dwt(a, wavelet, mode, axis) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/pywt/_dwt.py:182: in dwt cA, cD = dwt_single(data, wavelet, mode) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ pywt/_extensions/_dwt.pyx:26: in pywt._extensions._dwt.__pyx_fuse_1dwt_single ??? <stringsource>:663: in View.MemoryView.memoryview_cwrapper ??? _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
> ??? E ValueError: buffer source array is read-only
<stringsource>:351: ValueError

testcase.name: test_fpread

failure.message: ValueError: buffer source array is read-only

testcase.failure:
tmpdir = local('/private/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/pytest-of-nelsonmbigili/pytest-52/test_fpread0')
def test_fpread(tmpdir): """Test of writing and reading partitioning results."""
wue_data = { "meas_ht": 7.11, "canopy_ht": 4.42, "ppath": "C3", "ci_mod": "const_ratio", } fname = os.path.join( TESTDIR, "data/TOA5_6843.ts_Above_2012_06_07_1300.dat" ) > fvsp = fvs_partition( fname, wue_options=wue_data, hfd_format="ec-TOA5", interval="1min" )
tests/test_fluxpart.py:110: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ fluxpart/api.py:314: in fvs_partition return fvspart( fluxpart/fluxpart.py:304: in fvspart fluxes, fvsp = fvspart_progressive( fluxpart/partition.py:60: in fvspart_progressive for cnt, lowcut_wqc in enumerate(_progressive_lowcut(w, q, c)): fluxpart/partition.py:421: in _progressive_lowcut for lowcut_series in zip(lowcut_w, lowcut_q, lowcut_c): fluxpart/util.py:253: in progressive_lowcut_series decomp_coef = pywt.wavedec(series_data, wavelet=wavelet, level=nlevel) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/pywt/_multilevel.py:103: in wavedec a, d = dwt(a, wavelet, mode, axis) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/pywt/_dwt.py:182: in dwt cA, cD = dwt_single(data, wavelet, mode) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ pywt/_extensions/_dwt.pyx:26: in pywt._extensions._dwt.__pyx_fuse_1dwt_single ??? <stringsource>:663: in View.MemoryView.memoryview_cwrapper ??? _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
> ??? E ValueError: buffer source array is read-only
<stringsource>:351: ValueError

testcase.name: test_hfdata_reader

failure.message: TypeError: Invalid value '[False False True False False False False False False False False False]' for dtype 'int64'

testcase.failure:
self = <pandas.core.indexing._iLocIndexer object at 0x1120bbfc0>, loc = 7 value = array([False, False, True, False, False, False, False, False, False, False, False, False]) plane_indexer = slice(None, None, None)
def _setitem_single_column(self, loc: int, value, plane_indexer) -> None: """
Parameters ---------- loc : int Indexer for column position plane_indexer : int, slice, listlike[int] The indexer we use for setitem along axis=0. """ pi = plane_indexer
is_full_setter = com.is_null_slice(pi) or com.is_full_slice(pi, len(self.obj))
is_null_setter = com.is_empty_slice(pi) or (is_array_like(pi) and len(pi) == 0)
if is_null_setter: # no-op, don't cast dtype later return
elif is_full_setter: try: > self.obj._mgr.column_setitem( loc, plane_indexer, value, inplace_only=True )
.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/pandas/core/indexing.py:2144: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/pandas/core/internals/managers.py:1518: in column_setitem col_mgr.setitem_inplace(idx, value) .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/pandas/core/internals/managers.py:2220: in setitem_inplace value = np_can_hold_element(arr.dtype, value) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
dtype = dtype('int64') element = array([False, False, True, False, False, False, False, False, False, False, False, False])
def np_can_hold_element(dtype: np.dtype, element: Any) -> Any: """ Raise if we cannot losslessly set this element into an ndarray with this dtype.
Specifically about places where we disagree with numpy. i.e. there are cases where numpy will raise in doing the setitem that we do not check for here, e.g. setting str "X" into a numeric ndarray.
Returns ------- Any The element, potentially cast to the dtype.
Raises ------ ValueError : If we cannot losslessly store this element with this dtype. """ if dtype == _dtype_obj: return element
tipo = _maybe_infer_dtype_type(element)
if dtype.kind in "iu": if isinstance(element, range): if _dtype_can_hold_range(element, dtype): return element raise LossySetitemError
if is_integer(element) or (is_float(element) and element.is_integer()): # e.g. test_setitem_series_int8 if we have a python int 1 # tipo may be np.int32, despite the fact that it will fit # in smaller int dtypes. info = np.iinfo(dtype) if info.min <= element <= info.max: return dtype.type(element) raise LossySetitemError
if tipo is not None: if tipo.kind not in "iu": if isinstance(element, np.ndarray) and element.dtype.kind == "f": # If all can be losslessly cast to integers, then we can hold them with np.errstate(invalid="ignore"): # We check afterwards if cast was losslessly, so no need to show # the warning casted = element.astype(dtype) comp = casted == element if comp.all(): # Return the casted values bc they can be passed to # np.putmask, whereas the raw values cannot. # see TestSetitemFloatNDarrayIntoIntegerSeries return casted raise LossySetitemError
elif isinstance(element, ABCExtensionArray) and isinstance( element.dtype, CategoricalDtype ): # GH#52927 setting Categorical value into non-EA frame # TODO: general-case for EAs? try: casted = element.astype(dtype) except (ValueError, TypeError) as err: raise LossySetitemError from err # Check for cases of either # a) lossy overflow/rounding or # b) semantic changes like dt64->int64 comp = casted == element if not comp.all(): raise LossySetitemError return casted
# Anything other than integer we cannot hold > raise LossySetitemError E pandas.errors.LossySetitemError
.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/pandas/core/dtypes/cast.py:1725: LossySetitemError
The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:
def test_hfdata_reader(): toy_data = ( "foobar baz\n" "'asdf',0,2,3,4,5,6,7,9,0\n" "'asdf',1,2,3,4,5,6,7,9,0\n" "'asdf',2,2,3,4,5,6,7,9,1\n" "'asdf',3,2,3,4,5,6,,9,0\n" "'asdf',4,2,3,4,5,6,7,9,0\n" "# foo\n" "'asdf',5,2,3,4,5,6,7,9,0\n" "'asdf',6,2,3,4,5,6,7,xxx,0\n" "'asdf',7,???,3,4,5,6,7,9,0\n" "'asdf',8,2,3,4,5,6,7,9,0\n" "'asdf',9,2,3,4,5,6,7,9,0\n" "'asdf',10,2,3,4,5,6,7,9,0\n" "'asdf',11,-2,3,4,5,6,7,9,0\n" )
source = HFDataSource( files=[io.BytesIO(toy_data.encode())], filetype="csv", cols=(1, 2, 3, 6, 7, 4, 5), comment="#", skiprows=1, na_values="???", converters={"q": _converter_func(10., 0)}, flags=(9, 0), delimiter=",", )
> toy = HFData(next(source.reader(interval=None))) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_hfdata.py:45: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ fluxpart/hfdata.py:333: in reader yield from util.chunked_df(hf_dfs, interval) fluxpart/util.py:35: in chunked_df for df in dataframes: fluxpart/hfdata.py:331: in <genexpr> hf_dfs = (self._set_flags(df) for df in si_dfs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ fluxpart/hfdata.py:365: in _set_flags df.loc[:, "flag-" + str(col)] = ( ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/pandas/core/indexing.py:938: in __setitem__ iloc._setitem_with_indexer(indexer, value, self.name) .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/pandas/core/indexing.py:1953: in _setitem_with_indexer self._setitem_with_indexer_split_path(indexer, value, name) .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/pandas/core/indexing.py:1997: in _setitem_with_indexer_split_path self._setitem_single_column(ilocs[0], value, pi) _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <pandas.core.indexing._iLocIndexer object at 0x1120bbfc0>, loc = 7 value = array([False, False, True, False, False, False, False, False, False, False, False, False]) plane_indexer = slice(None, None, None)
def _setitem_single_column(self, loc: int, value, plane_indexer) -> None: """
Parameters ---------- loc : int Indexer for column position plane_indexer : int, slice, listlike[int] The indexer we use for setitem along axis=0. """ pi = plane_indexer
is_full_setter = com.is_null_slice(pi) or com.is_full_slice(pi, len(self.obj))
is_null_setter = com.is_empty_slice(pi) or (is_array_like(pi) and len(pi) == 0)
if is_null_setter: # no-op, don't cast dtype later return
elif is_full_setter: try: self.obj._mgr.column_setitem( loc, plane_indexer, value, inplace_only=True ) except (ValueError, TypeError, LossySetitemError) as exc: # If we're setting an entire column and we can't do it inplace, # then we can use value's dtype (or inferred dtype) # instead of object dtype = self.obj.dtypes.iloc[loc] if dtype not in (np.void, object) and not self.obj.empty: # - Exclude np.void, as that is a special case for expansion. # We want to raise for # df = pd.DataFrame({'a': [1, 2]}) # df.loc[:, 'a'] = .3 # but not for # df = pd.DataFrame({'a': [1, 2]}) # df.loc[:, 'b'] = .3 # - Exclude `object`, as then no upcasting happens. # - Exclude empty initial object with enlargement, # as then there's nothing to be inconsistent with. > raise TypeError( f"Invalid value '{value}' for dtype '{dtype}'" ) from exc E TypeError: Invalid value '[False False True False False False False False False False False False]' for dtype 'int64'
.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/pandas/core/indexing.py:2163: TypeError
